name: cortex-app
description: Tauri desktop app — an autonomous Claude-powered agent with a single goal (grow revenue/MRR), KPI tracking, and unrestricted action space

userStories.title: Scaffold Tauri + React app with dev tooling
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a working Tauri app shell with React frontend so that I have a foundation to build on
acceptanceCriteria: Tauri app launches a window with React frontend rendering
Rust backend compiles with cargo check
Frontend builds with pnpm build
Hot reload works in dev mode (pnpm tauri dev)
Project structure follows Tauri v2 conventions (src-tauri/ for Rust, src/ for React)
TypeScript configured with strict mode
Tailwind CSS installed and working
e2eTests: App window opens without crash
React root renders a placeholder screen
userStories.notes: Use Tauri v2. React + TypeScript + Tailwind. Vite as bundler.

userStories.title: Goal configuration — set a single goal with target and deadline
userStories.description: As a user, I want to define one goal (e.g., 'Grow MRR to $10K/mo by June') so the agent knows exactly what to optimize for
acceptanceCriteria: Goal setup screen with fields: goal name, target metric value, current value, deadline
Goal persists to local storage (Tauri fs or SQLite)
Only one active goal at a time — setting a new goal archives the previous one
Goal displays prominently on the main dashboard
Validation: target must be numeric, deadline must be future date
e2eTests: User can create a goal and see it on the dashboard
Goal persists after app restart
userStories.notes: Keep it dead simple — one goal, one number, one deadline. The agent's entire existence revolves around this.

userStories.title: KPI definition and manual data input
userStories.description: As a user, I want to define KPIs that track progress toward my goal and manually enter data points so the agent can measure its impact
acceptanceCriteria: User can add multiple KPIs (e.g., MRR, signups, churn rate, conversion rate)
Each KPI has: name, unit, current value, target value, direction (up/down is good)
Manual data entry form: select KPI, enter value, timestamp auto-filled
Data points stored locally with full history
KPI list view shows current value, trend arrow, % to target
e2eTests: User can add a KPI and enter a data point
KPI list displays correct trend direction
userStories.notes: MVP is manual input only. Future: Stripe API, analytics integrations.

userStories.title: KPI dashboard with trend visualization
userStories.description: As a user, I want a dashboard showing my goal progress and KPI trends over time so I can see if the agent is working
acceptanceCriteria: Main dashboard shows: goal with progress bar, time remaining, projected completion
Sparkline charts for each KPI showing last 30 data points
Color coding: green (on track), yellow (behind), red (off track)
Summary stat: overall goal health score (composite of KPI trends)
Dashboard auto-refreshes when new data is entered
e2eTests: Dashboard renders with goal and KPI data
Charts display data points correctly
Color coding matches KPI status
userStories.notes: Use a lightweight chart lib (e.g., recharts or visx). Keep it clean and glanceable.

userStories.title: Claude API agent brain with goal-directed reasoning
userStories.description: As the system, I want a Claude API integration that reasons about the goal, KPIs, and available actions so the agent can plan intelligently
acceptanceCriteria: Rust backend makes Claude API calls via anthropic SDK or HTTP
System prompt includes: current goal, KPI snapshot, action history, constraints
Agent can reason about which actions would most impact KPIs
Conversation history maintained per session (context window management)
API key stored securely in Tauri keychain/credential store
Error handling: rate limits, network failures, token limits
e2eTests: Agent brain receives goal context and returns a structured action plan
API key validation works on setup
userStories.notes: Use Claude's tool_use for structured action output. The agent should return actions as tool calls, not free text.

userStories.title: Autonomous action loop — plan, act, measure cycle
userStories.description: As the agent, I want to run an autonomous loop that plans actions, executes them, and measures results so I can continuously pursue the goal
acceptanceCriteria: Agent loop runs on configurable interval (default: every 4 hours)
Each cycle: (1) read goal + KPIs, (2) review action history, (3) plan next actions, (4) execute, (5) log results
Loop runs in background (Tauri background task / Rust async)
User can pause/resume the loop from the UI
Each cycle produces an action log entry with: timestamp, reasoning, actions taken, results
Loop respects a daily action budget (configurable max actions per day)
e2eTests: Agent loop completes one full cycle without error
Pause/resume controls work correctly
Action log captures cycle details
userStories.notes: This is the heart of cortex. The loop must be resilient — crash recovery, retry logic, graceful degradation.

userStories.title: Action framework — extensible system for agent to execute actions
userStories.description: As the agent, I want an extensible action framework so I can execute diverse actions (write content, run shell commands, call APIs, create files)
acceptanceCriteria: Action trait/interface: each action has name, description, execute(), validate_result()
Built-in actions for MVP: shell_command, write_file, http_request, create_content
Agent selects actions via Claude tool_use — each action is a tool
Action results feed back into agent context for next cycle
Sandboxing: actions run in a restricted context (no arbitrary system access without user-approved permissions)
Action registry: new actions can be added as plugins
e2eTests: Agent can execute a write_file action and verify the output exists
Action registry lists all available actions
Sandboxing prevents unauthorized system access
userStories.notes: The action space is deliberately unrestricted — the agent decides what to do. Permissions model: user approves action categories on first use, then they're allowed going forward.

userStories.title: Activity feed and cycle reporting
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see what the agent has been doing — its reasoning, actions, and results — so I can review its work async
acceptanceCriteria: Activity feed shows chronological list of agent cycles
Each cycle entry shows: timestamp, goal context, reasoning summary, actions taken, results, KPI impact
Expandable detail view for full agent reasoning chain
Filter by: action type, time range, success/failure
Notification badge when new cycles complete
e2eTests: Activity feed displays completed cycles
Filter controls narrow results correctly
Detail view shows full reasoning chain
userStories.notes: This is the primary way the user reviews the agent's work. Make reasoning transparent.

userStories.title: E2E test infrastructure for Tauri app
userStories.description: As a developer, I want E2E testing infrastructure so that I can verify the full app works end-to-end
acceptanceCriteria: Tauri E2E test harness configured (WebDriver or Playwright with Tauri)
At least one smoke test: app launches, goal can be set, KPI entered
CI-compatible: tests can run headless
Test fixtures for goal and KPI data
cargo test runs Rust unit tests, pnpm test runs frontend tests
e2eTests: E2E smoke test passes: launch → set goal → enter KPI → verify dashboard
userStories.notes: Phase 0 testing story. Tauri E2E is tricky — consider using tauri-driver or WebDriverIO.

metadata.goal: Build an autonomous desktop agent that pursues a single goal (revenue/MRR growth) with KPI tracking, Claude-powered reasoning, and an unrestricted action space
metadata.successCriteria: Agent can run autonomously for 24 hours — completing multiple plan/act/measure cycles, taking real actions, and showing KPI trend data on the dashboard
